name: Methane hydrate three-phase (Lw-H-V) equilibrium
source: Deaton, W.M. & Frost, E.M. (1946), 'Gas Hydrates and Their Relation to the Operation of Natural-Gas Pipe Lines', U.S. Bureau of Mines Monograph 8 (PRIMARY/origin). Reproduced and corroborated in Sloan, E.D. & Koh, C.A. (2008), 'Clathrate Hydrates of Natural Gases', 3rd ed., CRC Press (Appendix A) — the standard, universally-cited compilation. The 274.7 K / 3.27 MPa pure-methane point is independently corroborated by the Blake Ridge equilibrium measurement (ODP Leg 164). These methane Lw-H-V points are public-domain and appear in essentially every gas-hydrate text and the NIST/standard references.
method: isochoric / isothermal pressure-search dissociation; three-phase (liquid water - hydrate - vapour) equilibrium boundary
units: points are [P_bar, T_C]; P in bar absolute, T in degrees Celsius
